reasons_to_visit.details: Lake Geneva at 22–23°C is warm enough for proper swimming. Bains des Pâquis, Plage des Eaux-Vives, and Genève-Plage hum from morning to midnight.
Musiques en été, OpenAir Cinéma, and a packed Fête de la Musique afterglow mean free concerts and films almost every evening.

reasons_to_visit.reason: Long Hot Evenings on the Quais
reasons_to_visit.details: Sunsets near 21:30, lit-up Jet d'Eau against deep blue skies, and apéros on every quai stretch nightly social life into the small hours.
All Salève, Jura, Mont Blanc–area, and Vaud Alps trails are accessible. Day trips to Chamonix, Verbier, or Les Diablerets are easy from Geneva.
End of July sees Fêtes de Genève sound-checks and the city preparing for Switzerland's August 1 National Day fireworks and parades.
Conference and UN agency travel quiets in late July, freeing up better mid-week hotel deals than June or September.

reasons_to_reconsider.details: Heatwaves push afternoon temperatures past 32°C. Many Geneva hotels (especially Old Town and Pâquis townhouses) lack full AC; check before booking in heat-wave forecasts.
Reformation Wall, Bourg-de-Four, and Jet d'Eau jetty all see significant midday crowds, and the Mouettes water buses queue at peak times.
Heatwaves often end in dramatic Alpine thunderstorms — occasionally cancelling lake activities or open-air concerts.
Geneva's beloved baths are wonderful but extremely busy on hot weekends — pre-9am or post-19:00 best.
Quai-side terraces ratchet up their summer tariffs; plan apéros at neighborhood bistros for value.
Grass pollen remains active early July; UV intense throughout.

weather.precipitation: Hot and largely dry, with occasional thunderstorms. Lake at 22°C and warming.
weather.general_tips: Sun protection, hydration, and a hotel with AC for heatwaves are essential.

tourism_level.crowds: Busy throughout. Weekends and bank holidays especially so.
tourism_level.pricing: Peak summer rates; lakefront properties at year-high.
tourism_level.overall_atmosphere: Mediterranean — Geneva tilts toward outdoor living, with the lake as the city's daytime and nighttime square.

events_holidays.name: Montreux Jazz Festival Begins
events_holidays.description: World-famous festival up the lake — many travellers base in Geneva and take train day trips.
events_holidays.notes: Train Geneva → Montreux runs 70 minutes; late return trains laid on during festival.

events_holidays.name: Paléo Festival (Nyon)
events_holidays.description: One of Europe's largest open-air music festivals, 25 minutes from Geneva by train.
events_holidays.notes: Day tickets sell out; many visitors stay in Geneva and commute.

events_holidays.name: OpenAir Cinema Continues
events_holidays.description: Free outdoor cinema along the lakefront.
events_holidays.notes: Bring layers; lake breeze cool after dark.

unique_experiences.description: Swim at 6:30 am as the city wakes — Mont Blanc lit pink, joggers along the quais, the lake at its calmest.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Bring towel and locker coin. Coffee and tartines at the buvette afterwards.

unique_experiences.description: Jazz, soul, rock, and electronic legends on multiple lakefront stages — many free fringe events too.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Combine paid main-stage ticket with free Music in the Park stages. Last train back leaves around 01:30 during festival.

unique_experiences.description: Geneva's flagship public beach: sandy shore, lawns, lake swimming, restaurant kiosks, and open-air cinema at night.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Free entry. Arrive by 10 to claim shaded spots.

unique_experiences.activity: Vineyard Hike to Russin & Cellar Lunch
unique_experiences.where: Geneva canton, west of the city
unique_experiences.description: Take the train to Russin, walk the rolling Mandement vineyards above the Rhône gorge, and lunch at a family vineyard restaurant.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Train 12 minutes from Cornavin. Book lunch in advance at vineyards like Domaine des Balisiers.

weather.precipitation: Hot, often with intense afternoon storms during heatwave breaks.
weather.general_tips: Plan morning activity windows in hot spells; evenings ideal for everything else.

tourism_level.crowds: Sustained peak. Fêtes de Genève sound-checks attract early festival traffic at end of month.
tourism_level.pricing: Peak. Some softening late month as business travel falls away.
tourism_level.overall_atmosphere: Pre-festival anticipation building toward Fêtes de Genève. The city's outdoor calendar is at full throttle.

events_holidays.name: Fêtes de Genève Build-Up
events_holidays.description: Stages, sound systems, and food markets installed along the lakefront for the city's main summer festival.
events_holidays.notes: Some quai-side traffic disruption; lake walks unaffected.

events_holidays.name: Paléo Festival Closing
events_holidays.description: Final big nights of Paléo, 25 minutes from Geneva by train.
events_holidays.notes: Buy single-day tickets in advance. Last trains laid on for festival-goers.

events_holidays.name: Bastille Day Spillover
events_holidays.description: French national day fireworks in nearby French border towns (Annemasse, Évian, Annecy). Cross-border visitors arrive in Geneva.
events_holidays.notes: Trains and buses to French border towns busy in the evening.

unique_experiences.activity: Sunset SUP from Bains des Pâquis
unique_experiences.description: Paddleboard north toward the Jet d'Eau just as the lights come on and Mont Blanc fades to pink.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Rentals available right at the baths. Stay close to shore; storms can build fast.

unique_experiences.where: CGN evening cruise from Quai du Mont-Blanc
unique_experiences.description: Long July dusks make a Friday or Saturday-evening dinner cruise particularly atmospheric.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Book dining slot in advance. Light evening jacket for top deck.

unique_experiences.description: Catch first cable car up at Chamonix and stand at 3,842m as light hits Mont Blanc.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Book first slot online. Start from Geneva at 5 am; allow full day.

unique_experiences.where: Cour de l'Hôtel de Ville, Parc La Grange
unique_experiences.description: Late-month closing nights bring some of the festival's best ensembles.
